rationale: Educ. Code §29.160 governs early childhood demonstration projects and coordination-of-resources pilots. It contains no homestead exemption, property tax, or revenue-trigger provisions that would conflict with raising the Tax Code §11.13(b) exemption amount.
quote: Sec. 29.160. DEMONSTRATION PROJECTS. (a) The State Center for Early Childhood Development, in conjunction with a school district, regional education service center, institution of higher education, local government, local workforce development board, or community organization, may develop a quality rating system demonstration project under which prekindergarten program providers, licensed child-care facilities, or Head Start and Early Head Start program providers are assessed under a quality rating system.
_note: No direct or indirect conflict identified. The statute addresses demonstration project procedures and does not reference homestead exemptions, property tax appraisals, or Tax Code §11.13(b).
